title: Kaggle GM Loop
description: Phase-aware campaign strategy mirroring the kaggle-gm-workflow skill. It advances through the 7-phase Kaggle GM playbook while still executing supported work as experiment rounds inside the existing agentic research runtime.

phases.title: Phase 1 - EDA & Baseline
phases.summary: Establish a payout-target LightGBM floor on scout-friendly data and verify the basic data/config setup is sane.
phases.gate: Baseline is trained, data issues are clear, and the campaign is ready to move into a diversity campaign.

phases.title: Phase 2 - Diversity Campaign
phases.summary: Expand across targets, model families, and feature scope to build a diverse set of promising candidates.
phases.gate: There is a credible diverse pool of candidates, not just one narrow local optimum.

phases.title: Phase 3 - HPO Refinement
phases.summary: Refine the strongest candidate families with controlled hyperparameter sweeps.
phases.gate: The best families have been tested for meaningful improvement over defaults.

phases.title: Phase 4 - Seed Ensembling
phases.summary: Reduce variance by testing multi-seed versions of the strongest candidates.
phases.gate: Seed-averaged candidates show a better stability profile than single-seed versions.

phases.title: Phase 5 - Ensemble Construction
phases.summary: Use the campaign learnings to define which candidates should be blended or stacked next.
phases.gate: There is a clear ensemble direction or a reason to stop instead of forcing one.

phases.title: Phase 6 - Post-Processing
phases.summary: Plan neutralization and prediction-shape checks that should be run on the leading candidates.
phases.gate: There is a defensible post-processing policy for the candidate set.

phases.title: Phase 7 - Validation & Submission
phases.summary: Decide whether the campaign is submission-ready or should stop for user review.
phases.gate: The campaign is ready for user review and optional submission. Do not auto-submit.
